preprocessing: Drop the customerID column since it is an identifier with no predictive value.
Convert the SeniorCitizen column to boolean type (0=False, 1=True).
Convert Partner, Dependents, PhoneService, PaperlessBilling, and Churn columns to boolean type by mapping 'Yes'/'No' strings to True/False.
Replace empty strings in TotalCharges with NaN and convert the column to float.
Fill or impute any remaining NaN values in TotalCharges, for example with 0 or using an appropriate strategy based on tenure=0.
Encode categorical columns with two categories (gender) using binary encoding (e.g., Male=0, Female=1).

Encode all remaining categorical columns with more than two categories (MultipleLines, InternetService, OnlineSecurity, OnlineBackup, DeviceProtection, TechSupport, StreamingTV, StreamingMovies, Contract, PaymentMethod) using one-hot encoding, dropping the first category to avoid collinearity.
Ensure tenure and MonthlyCharges columns are numeric types.
Save the fully preprocessed dataset as a CSV file in ./data/processed folder.
training: Read the preprocessed dataset from the ./data/processed folder.
Split the data into features (X) and target (y) using the Churn column as target.
Split the data into training and testing subsets with stratification on the Churn column to preserve class distribution.
Initialize a Logistic Regression model with appropriate class_weight='balanced' to combat moderate class imbalance.
Fit the Logistic Regression model using the training data.
Save the trained Logistic Regression model using joblib.
